items.title: USDA lists state SNAP waivers that restrict purchases of soda, candy, and other items
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service updated its SNAP Food Restriction Waivers list with multiple state waivers that restrict what can be bought with SNAP, including limits on soda/soft drinks and candy in several states. The list shows target implementation dates ranging from February 2026 to later dates (for example, Florida lists 04/20/26 and Nebraska lists 01/01/26, with additional Nebraska restrictions listed as effective 11/01/26).

items.title: CMS issues guidance on new limits to federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for certain noncitizens
items.summary: CMS released a State Health Official letter to prepare states for a statutory change that limits federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for certain noncitizens, focusing on eligibility systems, verification, and claims processing. CMS says the change begins October 1, 2026, and notes the change does not apply to state options to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant people, and that federal matching funds remain available for treatment of emergency medical conditions.

items.title: KFF survey highlights major upcoming Medicaid/CHIP eligibility and renewal changes (2026–2029)
items.summary: KFF’s annual survey of state Medicaid and CHIP officials summarizes multiple federal policy changes states are preparing for, including immigrant eligibility restrictions beginning October 1, 2026, and a requirement starting January 1, 2026 for states to suspend (not terminate) Medicaid for adults during incarceration. KFF also reports that starting January 2027, expansion states must implement work/community engagement requirements and semi-annual renewals for certain groups, and that later changes include mandatory cost-sharing of up to $35 per service for some ACA expansion adults beginning October 2028.

items.title: HUD updates verification requirements for Emergency Housing Voucher and Stability Voucher programs
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-05 removes waiver/alternative language that had allowed Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) admissions without initial Social Security number and eligible immigration status documentation, and directs PHAs to follow existing regulations for SSN disclosure/verification and citizenship/eligible immigration evidence. HUD says PHAs must be in compliance within 60 days of publication, including verifying eligible immigration status for new SV admissions and current EHV/SV families and ensuring SSNs for family members admitted during the period of availability are verified in EIV or otherwise documented.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development

items.title: IRS opened the 2026 tax filing season; EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2
items.summary: The IRS says it opened the 2026 filing season on January 26, 2026 and began accepting tax year 2025 individual returns, with a federal filing deadline of April 15. For filers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and the Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) who choose direct deposit, the IRS says most refunds should be available by March 2, 2026, and that its “Where’s My Refund?” tool will show projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: SSA: Social Security and SSI get a 2.8% COLA for 2026; SSI federal payment standard rises
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet reports a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment effective January 2026 for both Social Security benefits and SSI payments. The fact sheet lists the SSI federal payment standard at $994 per month for an individual and $1,491 per month for a couple, and sets the Social Security maximum taxable earnings at $184,500 for 2026.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash value benefit amounts
items.summary: USDA’s WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value benefit (CVB/CVV) amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully or mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA notes FY 2026 runs from Oct. 1, 2025 through Sept. 30, 2026.
